steps.title: Decode the variable and what the function represents
steps.content:
The variable $x$ represents the number of years after the end of 1954.

Since $x = 5$, we're looking at 5 years after the end of 1954, which is the end of 1959.

The function $f(x) = 250(1.035)^x$ models the VALUE of the bank account in dollars.

Therefore, when we evaluate $f(5) \approx 297$, we're finding the account value (in dollars) at $x = 5$, which is the end of 1959.

steps.title: Match the interpretation to the correct choice
steps.content:
Now we interpret what $f(5) \approx 297$ means: the account value is approximately \$297 at the end of 1959.
  • Choice A — The value of the bank account is estimated to be approximately 5 dollars greater at the end of 1959 than at the end of 1954.: 5 dollars greater — but $f(5)$ gives the total value, not a difference. Incorrect.
  • Choice B — The value of the bank account is estimated to be approximately 297 dollars at the end of 1959.: 297 dollars at the end of 1959 — this is exactly what $f(5) \approx 297$ tells us.
  • Choice C — The value, in dollars, of the bank account is estimated to be approximately 5 times greater at the end of 1959 than at the end of 1954.: 5 times greater — misreads $x = 5$ as a multiplier rather than a time unit. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — The value of the bank account is estimated to increase by approximately 297 dollars every 5 years between the end of 1954 and the end of 1969.: increase by 297 dollars every 5 years — confuses the total value with a rate of increase. Incorrect.
That confirms B is correct.
